2024 FCA 88
Federal Court of Appeal 2024
2024-05-21
Stratas J.A. Mactavish J.A. Goyette J.A.

Issues

1

This application for judicial review concerns a decision of the Parole Board of Canada revoking the applicant's statutory release pursuant to s. 135 of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act, S.C. 1992, c. 20.

2

The applicant raises three issues: (i) whether the Board's reasons disclose a defensible chain of analysis; (ii) whether the Board considered relevant mitigating factors; and (iii) whether the revocation was a proportionate response to the alleged breach of conditions.

Standard of Review

3

The applicable standard is reasonableness as articulated in Canada (Minister of Citizenship and Immigration) v. Vavilov, 2019 SCC 65. A reasonable decision is one that is based on an internally coherent and rational chain of analysis and justified in relation to the facts and law that constrain the decision-maker.

Analysis

4

We are not satisfied that the Board's reasons meet the threshold of justification, transparency, and intelligibility required of an administrative decision affecting liberty interests.

5

The Board failed to grapple with the applicant's evidence regarding his employment and accommodation. While the Board need not respond to every argument, the failure to engage with central pieces of evidence is fatal in this case.

6

Further, the Board's reasoning on proportionality is conclusory. The decision recites the relevant factors but does not explain how those factors weigh in the particular circumstances. We hold that this is precisely the deficiency that Vavilov warns against.

Disposition

7

The application for judicial review is allowed. The Board's decision is set aside and the matter is remitted for redetermination by a differently constituted panel.
